title: TRIED Eval and Training Example
description: Shared schema for both training and eval corpus examples. The agent reads the same structure regardless of split. Training examples live in data/corpus_train.jsonl; eval examples live in eval/holdout/corpus_eval.jsonl.

example_id.description: UUID. Generated once at extraction time. Stable across reruns of the same op.

split.description: Which corpus this example belongs to. Controls which file it is written to; the agent loop reads only 'train' examples.

difficulty.description: Difficulty tier. Always null for training examples. Required and non-null for eval examples. Easy: 2-op elementwise fusions. Medium: 3-op or any fusion involving a reduction. Hard: 4+ ops or complex memory patterns.

pytorch_code.description: Standalone PyTorch function the agent is asked to translate to Triton. Executable with import torch only, no module dependencies.

input_shapes.description: Shape of each input tensor, as a list of integer lists.

input_dtypes.description: Dtype of each input tensor. Length must match input_shapes.

rng_seed.description: RNG seed used to generate input tensors. Fixed per example for reproducibility.

op_category.description: Coarse op family. Closed vocabulary. Finalized after corpus extraction - do not add values without a decision-log entry and team sign-off.
